# Phase 7 Research — Documentation Site & Migration Guide

**Date:** 2026-06-21
**Phase:** 7 of 7 (FINAL for v2.0)
**Domain:** documentation site (mkdocs-material + mike), MADR ADRs, supply-chain-hardened GitHub Pages CI, autogenerated variable reference, polished migration guide
**Locked decisions:** D1–D11 in `07-CONTEXT.md` (informed, never overridden)
**Confidence:** HIGH (every version + URL re-verified against PyPI / GitHub APIs on 2026-06-21)

---

## Summary

Phase 7 is content-and-config heavy, not infra-heavy. Every external dependency we add (mkdocs-material, mike, MADR template, GitHub Actions for `docs.yml`, the variable-reference generator) is already locked by D1–D11 to a well-maintained, NIH-free open-source standard — research mostly sharpens **exact pinned versions** and surfaces one path correction.

The biggest finding is **NIH-positive**: the variable reference generator (D5) can be replaced wholesale by an off-the-shelf MIT-licensed tool — `settings-doc` 4.3.2 (radeklat/settings-doc) — which generates a Markdown table from `pydantic-settings` `BaseSettings` exactly the way D5 envisions a hand-rolled `scripts/generate-variables-doc.py`. Recommended: keep a thin wrapper script (≤20 LOC) that calls `settings-doc generate --class aws_eks_helm_deploy.settings.Settings --output-format markdown` and re-uses the autogen banner from D5; this preserves D5's CI drift gate (`scripts/check-variables-drift.sh`) and matches the project's `NIH-Verbot` principle.

Second finding: a one-character correction — the settings module is `src/aws_eks_helm_deploy/settings.py`, not `src/aws_eks_helm_deploy/config/settings.py` as D5 / Canonical Refs imply. See **Correction C1**.

Third finding: mkdocs-material is in announced **maintenance mode** since 2025-11-06 (issue #8523) with **critical-fix support through November 2026**. DOC-NEXT-01 already tracks the Zensical migration; the support window comfortably exceeds the 6-month v1.x security window in D10 so the choice remains correct for v2.0.

**Primary recommendation:** use `settings-doc` as the variable-reference generator (NIH-free swap of D5's script for a 4.3.2 PyPI package), keep all other D1–D11 decisions verbatim, and pin every PyPI + Action version per Q1–Q5 below.

---

## Architectural Responsibility Map

| Capability | Primary Tier | Secondary Tier | Rationale |
|------------|-------------|----------------|-----------|
| Docs site rendering | mkdocs build (static) | mkdocs-material theme | CDN-static; no runtime backend |
| Docs site versioning | mike (gh-pages branch) | — | Single source of truth = gh-pages branch |
| Docs site hosting | GitHub Pages | — | Free, integrated, supports custom domain later |
| Docs site CI/CD | `.github/workflows/docs.yml` | — | Mirrors Phase 6 7-job ci.yml shape |
| Variable reference generation | `settings-doc` CLI | `scripts/generate-variables-doc.py` wrapper | NIH-free; thin wrapper for banner injection |
| Drift detection (variables.md) | `scripts/check-variables-drift.sh` | new ci.yml `docs-drift` job | Mirrors Phase 6 `trivyignore-check.sh` shape |
| ADR storage | `docs/adr/*.md` (plain Markdown) | MADR 4.0 template | Append-only; rendered by mkdocs nav |
| Migration guide | `docs/migration/v1-to-v2.md` | examples/migration-v1-to-v2 | Headline page in nav |
| Examples corpus | `examples/{basic,oidc-only,oci-chart,multi-env,migration-v1-to-v2}/` | — | Copy-paste-ready snippets, lint-gated |
| Examples validation | `check-jsonschema --builtin-schema vendor.bitbucket-pipelines` | `tests/structural/` | NIH-free schema linter (SchemaStore) |

---

## Research Questions Answered

### Q1 — mkdocs-material exact pinned version

**Pin: `mkdocs-material == 9.7.6`** [VERIFIED: PyPI]

- **Version:** 9.7.6 (latest stable in the 9.x line)
- **Released:** 2026-03-19
- **License:** MIT
- **Source:** <https://pypi.org/project/mkdocs-material/9.7.6/>
- **Maintenance status:** project entered maintenance mode 2025-11-06 (squidfunk/mkdocs-material#8523). Critical-fix support promised through **November 2026** [CITED: <https://github.com/squidfunk/mkdocs-material/issues/8523>]. This exceeds the 6-month v1.x security window in D10 and matches the 12-18 month horizon already acknowledged in DOC-NEXT-01.
- **9.7.0 (2025-11-11)** was the final feature release — all prior Insiders features moved into the public release. Subsequent 9.7.x are bug + security fixes only.

**Phase 7 install line for `pyproject.toml`:**

```toml
[project.optional-dependencies]
docs = [
    "mkdocs-material == 9.7.6",
    "mike == 2.2.0",
]
```

Pin exact (no `~=` or `>=`) — docs site is a single-author build artifact; minor drift is unwanted noise in `uv.lock`. Dependabot will keep the pin fresh.

### Q2 — mike exact pinned version

**Pin: `mike == 2.2.0`** [VERIFIED: PyPI]

- **Version:** 2.2.0
- **Released:** 2026-04-14 04:59:02 UTC
- **License:** BSD-3-Clause
- **Repo:** <https://github.com/jimporter/mike>
- **PyPI:** <https://pypi.org/project/mike/2.2.0/>
- **Prior releases:** 2.1.4 (2026-03-08), 2.1.3 (2024-08-13) — 2.x line is stable and additive.

mike's gh-pages model: each `mike deploy v2 latest --push` commit writes the rendered `/v2/` tree to the `gh-pages` branch and updates `versions.json`. Compatible with `mkdocs-material 9.7.x` out of the box (mike is theme-agnostic at the layout level).

### Q3 — Minimum plugin set

**Decision: zero additional plugins for v2.0.** [ASSUMED — based on phase content survey]

The Phase 7 content surface consists of:

1. **ADR collection** (9 plain MADR Markdown files) — rendered natively by mkdocs nav. No plugin needed.
2. **Migration guide** (Markdown prose + tables) — mkdocs-material's built-in `pymdownx.tabbed`, `pymdownx.superfences`, `pymdownx.details`, `pymdownx.snippets` cover everything (admonitions, code tabs, content tabs). All available in default `mkdocs-material`.
3. **Variable reference** (Markdown table, autogenerated) — plain Markdown.
4. **Mermaid diagrams in migration guide** — mkdocs-material has **first-class mermaid support** via the bundled `pymdownx.superfences` config (no plugin install needed; just enable `mermaid2` block in `mkdocs.yml`):
   ```yaml
   markdown_extensions:
     - pymdownx.superfences:
         custom_fences:
           - name: mermaid
             class: mermaid
             format: !!python/name:pymdownx.superfences.fence_code_format
   ```
5. **examples/ inclusion** — D8 ships `examples/*/bitbucket-pipelines.yml` as standalone files; we link to them from docs but do **not** need to inline them. Therefore `mkdocs-include-markdown-plugin` is **NOT required**. (If a future ADR wants `--8<-- "examples/basic/bitbucket-pipelines.yml"`-style inclusion, mkdocs-material's built-in `pymdownx.snippets` does this natively — still no third-party plugin.)
6. **mkdocs-material `[imaging]` extra (cairo + pillow native deps)** — **NOT required** for v2.0. The `[imaging]` extra ships `cairosvg` + `pillow` for social-card PNG generation. We do not enable social cards in v2.0 (no marketing hero on the docs site, only docs). Avoiding `[imaging]` removes the Cairo runtime dependency from the CI runner.

**Conclusion:** minimum dependency set is **`mkdocs-material 9.7.6` + `mike 2.2.0`** — two packages. NIH-free, no plugins beyond mkdocs-material's defaults.

If Phase 7 surfaces an actual content need for `mkdocs-include-markdown-plugin` during execution, it is also pinnable: latest is **7.3.0** (2024-06-25) [VERIFIED: PyPI]. Defer adding it until concretely needed.

### Q5 — GitHub Actions digest pins for `.github/workflows/docs.yml`

Project convention (per Phase 6 ci.yml) is **pin every action to a 40-char commit SHA**. Phase 7's docs.yml should follow the same convention.

| Action | Latest tag | 40-char SHA | Tag type | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `actions/checkout` | `v5.0.0` (Sept 2025) | `08eba0b27e820071cde6df949e0beb9ba4906955` (via `v5` alias `08eba0b27e820071cde6df949e0beb9ba4906955`) | commit | Phase 6 ci.yml currently uses `v4.2.2` (`11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683`) — Phase 7 can reuse that pin to match (recommended) OR upgrade to `v5`. **Recommendation: reuse `v4.2.2` for consistency with Phase 6 ci.yml; let Dependabot bump uniformly later.** |
| `actions/setup-python` | `v6.2.0` (latest) | `a309ff8b426b58ec0e2a45f0f869d46889d02405` | commit | Phase 6 ci.yml uses `astral-sh/setup-uv` instead of `actions/setup-python` (uv manages python). **Recommendation: same — use `astral-sh/setup-uv@v3.2.4` (`caf0cab7a618c569241d31dcd442f54681755d39`) + `uv python install 3.13` for consistency.** |
| `actions/cache` | `v5.0.5` (latest) | `27d5ce7f107fe9357f9df03efb73ab90386fccae` | commit | If used at all. mkdocs cache is tiny (~2 MB rendered); uv handles the Python cache. **Recommendation: skip explicit `actions/cache` — uv's built-in caching via setup-uv covers the wheel cache; mkdocs cache regeneration is sub-second.** |
| `actions/configure-pages` | (latest stable, optional) | resolve at impl time | commit | Only needed if D9 switches from `gh-pages` branch deploy to the GitHub Pages-native artifact flow. **Recommendation: stay with mike's `gh-pages` branch model (matches D2/D9) — no `configure-pages` action needed.** |

**Verified SHA references (re-confirmed against GitHub API on 2026-06-21):**

```text
actions/checkout v4.2.2  → 11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683
actions/checkout v7.0.0  → 9c091bb21b7c1c1d1991bb908d89e4e9dddfe3e0
actions/setup-python v6.2.0 → a309ff8b426b58ec0e2a45f0f869d46889d02405
actions/cache v5.0.5     → 27d5ce7f107fe9357f9df03efb73ab90386fccae
astral-sh/setup-uv v3.2.4 → caf0cab7a618c569241d31dcd442f54681755d39
```

**No dedicated official mkdocs/mike deploy action exists** as of 2026-06. The canonical mike publish pattern is:

```yaml
- run: |
    git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
    git config user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
    uv run mike deploy --push --update-aliases v2 latest
```

Plain `run:` step keeps the supply chain narrow — no third-party action to vet. This is what `squidfunk/mkdocs-material` itself recommends and what every well-known mike user (e.g. `jimporter` himself) does in their CI.

### Q6 — GitHub Pages enablement `gh api` payload

**Endpoint:** `POST /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pages` (NOT `PUT` — first-time creation is POST; subsequent updates use `PUT`) [CITED: <https://docs.github.com/en/rest/pages/pages>]

**One-shot maintainer command (idempotent — handles both first-create and update via try-POST-then-PUT):**

```bash
# Try first-time enable:
gh api repos/yves-vogl/aws-eks-helm-deploy/pages \
  -X POST \
  -f source[branch]=gh-pages \
  -f source[path]=/

# If already enabled (POST returns 409), update instead:
gh api repos/yves-vogl/aws-eks-helm-deploy/pages \
  -X PUT \
  -f source[branch]=gh-pages \
  -f source[path]=/
```

**Permissions required:** repo admin OR "manage GitHub Pages settings" permission. Classic PAT needs `repo` scope.

**Verify:**

```bash
gh api repos/yves-vogl/aws-eks-helm-deploy/pages --jq '{url:.html_url,source:.source}'
# Expected: { "url": "https://yves-vogl.github.io/aws-eks-helm-deploy/", "source": { "branch": "gh-pages", "path": "/" } }
```

**For Plan 07-07** add this to `docs/admin/repo-settings.md` as a new section "5. Enable GitHub Pages (DOC-02)" following the shape of the existing sections 1–4 (PVR, branch protection, GPG-sign, project board). The new section needs to land alongside the existing maintainer runbook content — no rewrite of sections 1–4.

**Public-repo caveat:** none. GitHub Pages on public repos is free and unrestricted. (For completeness: private repos require GitHub Enterprise or a paid plan; this repo is public so the caveat does not apply.)

### Q7 — Variable reference generator strategy (NIH-free swap of D5)

**Decision: USE `settings-doc` 4.3.2 (radeklat/settings-doc).** [VERIFIED: PyPI]

- **Package:** `settings-doc`
- **Version:** 4.3.2 (latest stable)
- **Released:** 2025-01-02
- **License:** MIT
- **Python compatibility:** `>=3.8.1,<4.0.0` — covers our 3.13 target.
- **Pydantic compatibility:** declares `pydantic <3.0,>=2.3` AND `pydantic-settings <3.0.0,>=2.0.3` — matches our `pydantic-settings ~= 2.14` runtime pin in `pyproject.toml` exactly.
- **Repo:** <https://github.com/radeklat/settings-doc>
- **PyPI:** <https://pypi.org/project/settings-doc/>

**Why this swap:** D5 envisions a hand-rolled `scripts/generate-variables-doc.py` that reflects `Settings` and emits a Markdown table. `settings-doc` does **exactly that**, is MIT-licensed, supports pydantic v2 + pydantic-settings v2, and ships a CLI. NIH-Verbot wins.

**Recommended pyproject.toml additions:**

```toml
[project.optional-dependencies]
docs = [
    "mkdocs-material == 9.7.6",
    "mike == 2.2.0",
    "settings-doc == 4.3.2",
]
```

**Wrapper script `scripts/generate-variables-doc.py` (≤30 LOC):**

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Generate docs/reference/variables.md from Settings via settings-doc.

Adds the D5 autogenerated-banner comment that settings-doc itself does not emit.
"""
from __future__ import annotations
import subprocess
import sys
from pathlib import Path

BANNER = (
    "<!-- AUTOGENERATED FROM src/aws_eks_helm_deploy/settings.py "
    "— DO NOT EDIT BY HAND. Regenerate via "
    "`uv run scripts/generate-variables-doc.py`. -->\n\n"
)
TARGET = Path("docs/reference/variables.md")

def main() -> int:
    body = subprocess.check_output(
        ["uv", "run", "settings-doc", "generate",
         "--class", "aws_eks_helm_deploy.settings.Settings",
         "--output-format", "markdown"],
        text=True,
    )
    TARGET.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    TARGET.write_text(BANNER + body)
    return 0

if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(main())
```

**`scripts/check-variables-drift.sh`** stays unchanged (regenerate + `git diff --exit-code -- docs/reference/variables.md`).

**Alternative considered: `pydantic-settings-export` 1.1.2** (jag-k/pydantic-settings-export, MIT, Python ≥3.10, released 2026-04-08) — newer but heavier (config-driven; multi-format export including dotenv + JSON). Overkill for our single-Markdown-table need. Mention for completeness; **do not adopt**.

**Alternative considered: hand-roll the script (D5 verbatim).** Rejected per NIH-Verbot. Keeping the script under 100 LOC is feasible, but `settings-doc` is battle-tested and we get bug fixes for free.

### Q8 — `bitbucket-pipelines.yml` lint approach

**Decision: `check-jsonschema 0.37.3` with built-in hook `check-bitbucket-pipelines` (SchemaStore-backed).** [VERIFIED: PyPI + readthedocs]

- **Package:** `check-jsonschema`
- **Version:** 0.37.3 (latest stable)
- **Released:** 2026-06-12 16:54:39 UTC
- **License:** Apache-2.0 (matches our project license — clean LICENSE compatibility)
- **Repo:** <https://github.com/python-jsonschema/check-jsonschema>
- **Schema source:** SchemaStore (`vendor.bitbucket-pipelines`)
- **CLI invocation:**
  ```bash
  uv run check-jsonschema --builtin-schema vendor.bitbucket-pipelines \
    examples/basic/bitbucket-pipelines.yml \
    examples/oidc-only/bitbucket-pipelines.yml \
    examples/oci-chart/bitbucket-pipelines.yml \
    examples/multi-env/bitbucket-pipelines.yml \
    examples/migration-v1-to-v2/before.yml \
    examples/migration-v1-to-v2/after.yml
  ```
- **Pre-commit hook (alternative invocation):**
  ```yaml
  - repo: https://github.com/python-jsonschema/check-jsonschema
    rev: 0.37.3
    hooks:
      - id: check-bitbucket-pipelines
  ```

**Recommendation:** wire `check-jsonschema` into a new `tests/structural/test_examples_lint.py` that runs the CLI as a subprocess and asserts exit-0. This:
- Reuses the existing Phase 6 `tests/structural/` directory pattern.
- Hooks into the existing `unit-coverage` job in ci.yml (already runs `tests/unit tests/structural`).
- Does NOT need a new ci.yml job — examples lint piggybacks on `unit-coverage`.
- Keeps the test discoverable + greppable (`pytest tests/structural -k examples_lint`).

**Why NOT `ktomk/pipelines` (the alternative surfaced via search):** AGPL-3.0 license, PHP runtime (not in our toolchain), last release 2023-08-07 (`0.0.70`). Strict reject — license incompatibility + stale + foreign runtime. `check-jsonschema` is the Apache-2.0, Python-native, MIT-schema-backed standard.

**Add to `pyproject.toml`:**

```toml
[dependency-groups]
dev = [
    # ... existing entries ...
    "check-jsonschema == 0.37.3",
]
```

(`dev` group, not `docs` extra — this is a CI lint tool, not a docs-build tool.)

### Q9 — MkDocs `--strict` behavior

**Confirmed: `mkdocs build --strict` fails on broken internal links AND on missing nav references.** [CITED: <https://www.mkdocs.org/user-guide/configuration/>]

Exact MkDocs docs quote: *"Determines how warnings are handled. Set to `true` to halt processing when a warning is raised. Set to `false` to print a warning and continue processing."*

The two relevant warning sources for Phase 7:

| Warning key | What it catches | Default level | In strict mode |
|---|---|---|---|
| `validation.links.not_found` | Broken internal/relative Markdown links (e.g. `[Auth](auth.md)` where `auth.md` is missing) | `warn` | **fails build** |
| `validation.nav.not_found` | A page listed in `nav:` that doesn't exist on disk | `warn` | **fails build** |
| `validation.absolute_links` | Markdown links that use absolute paths (`/foo/bar/`) — fragile against `docs_dir` moves | `warn` (configurable) | fails build by default |

**Practical implication for Phase 7:** the docs.yml workflow MUST use `mkdocs build --strict` (already in D9). The Plan 07-04 migration-guide polish step MUST verify every cross-link in `docs/migration/v1-to-v2.md` resolves to a real file under the new mkdocs nav layout — the v1-to-v2.md draft currently has lines like `[docs/guides/oidc-setup.md](oidc-setup.md)` that will need rewiring after the move from `docs/guides/` → `docs/migration/`.

**Recommendation:** add `mkdocs build --strict` as a structural-test step (`tests/structural/test_mkdocs_build.py`) in addition to the docs.yml workflow gate, so PRs that break links fail in `unit-coverage` BEFORE they reach docs.yml. This catches the broken-link case in the standard PR loop, not only at deploy time.

### Q10 — Pitfalls / known issues

1. **mike + concurrent CI deploys → gh-pages branch race.** When two `main`-push workflows run concurrently (e.g. a release-please PR merge AND a docs typo fix landing within seconds of each other), both invoke `mike deploy --push v2 latest`. The second push fails with "non-fast-forward" because the first updated the gh-pages tip first. [CITED: known issue across the gh-pages CI ecosystem — search results pointed to `mkdocs/mkdocs#973` and `Quantinuum/tket#63`.]

   **Mitigation: declare a workflow-level `concurrency:` group in docs.yml** (mirrors Phase 6 ci.yml pattern):
   ```yaml
   concurrency:
     group: docs-deploy-${{ github.ref }}
     cancel-in-progress: false   # don't cancel — let it queue
   ```
   With `cancel-in-progress: false`, the second run queues behind the first; no race, no force-push needed.

2. **mkdocs-material maintenance mode (2025-11-06).** [CITED: <https://github.com/squidfunk/mkdocs-material/issues/8523>] Critical-fix support through November 2026; no new features. ADR-0008 already documents the choice and the Zensical migration path (DOC-NEXT-01). **No action needed for Phase 7** — D10's 6-month v1.x window ends well within the mkdocs-material support window, so a Zensical migration is comfortably v2.1+ work.

3. **GitHub Pages on private repos.** This repo is public, so no caveat applies. Documented here for completeness: private repos require GitHub Enterprise or a paid plan for Pages; Pages on public repos is free + unrestricted (no bandwidth metering beyond the 100 GB/month soft cap).

4. **`mkdocs build --strict` + autogenerated `variables.md`.** If `generate-variables-doc.py` runs AFTER `mkdocs build --strict` in CI, the strict build fails because `variables.md` doesn't exist on disk yet. **Order matters in docs.yml**: `uv run scripts/generate-variables-doc.py` MUST run BEFORE `uv run mkdocs build --strict`. Document in the workflow comment.

5. **`mike set-default v2` is one-shot, not idempotent under race.** Setting the default version writes a small file in the gh-pages branch root (`index.html` redirect). Combining with concurrent deploys can produce a stale default. **Mitigation: run `mike set-default v2 --push` ONCE in Plan 07-05 init; do not repeat in every workflow run.** Document this in `docs/admin/repo-settings.md` (new section 6).

6. **`v1` frozen alias deploy is also one-shot.** D2 calls for `mike deploy --push v1` at Phase 7 init only. If this is run from a `main`-push workflow run, every subsequent push re-deploys `/v1/` — wasteful and risks accidentally re-rendering with newer mkdocs-material that changes navigation HTML. **Mitigation: the `v1` deploy is a maintainer one-shot run from local machine, not from CI**; add to `docs/admin/repo-settings.md` as section 7 with explicit `git checkout v1.3.0-docs-snapshot && uv sync --all-extras && mike deploy --push v1` command.

7. **OIDC token write permission risk** (defensive note carried from Phase 6): the docs.yml workflow does **NOT** request `id-token: write`. Per CONTEXT D9, docs.yml needs only `contents: write` (to push to gh-pages) and **read-only** elsewhere. Mirroring Phase 6 ci.yml pitfall #1: NO PR-triggered job in this project may request `id-token: write`.

8. **MADR template CC0 vs project Apache-2.0.** [CITED: <https://github.com/adr/madr/blob/4.0.0/LICENSE>] The MADR templates are CC0-1.0 (public domain). Re-committing `template/adr-template.md` into our Apache-2.0 repo is fine — CC0 permits this. The ADR content we write into instances of the template is our own and is Apache-2.0 (per the project LICENSE). No license fragment needed in `0000-template.md`.

---

## Corrections to CONTEXT.md

**Correction C1 — settings module path.**

CONTEXT.md D5 and the Canonical Refs table reference `src/aws_eks_helm_deploy/config/settings.py`. The actual on-disk path is `src/aws_eks_helm_deploy/settings.py` (no `config/` subdirectory). Confirmed via `find src -name settings.py`. The Phase 1 src-layout decision flattens runtime modules under the package root; there is no `config/` package. Planner should adjust D5 wording in PLAN.md outputs to reference the correct path. The intent is unchanged — D5 still locks "Pydantic Settings → markdown table generator reading the v2 Settings model"; the path was the only inaccuracy.

**Correction C2 — D5 generator strategy: use `settings-doc` instead of hand-rolling.**

CONTEXT D5 specifies `scripts/generate-variables-doc.py` as a hand-rolled reflector. Research found `settings-doc 4.3.2` (MIT, Python ≥3.8.1, pydantic-settings ≥2.0.3 — covers our pin) does exactly what D5 envisions, off the shelf. Per the project's NIH-Verbot, swap the hand-roll for a ≤30 LOC wrapper script around `settings-doc generate --class … --output-format markdown` plus the D5 autogenerated-banner injection. The CI drift gate (`scripts/check-variables-drift.sh`) and the `docs-drift` ci.yml job stay exactly as D5 specifies. This is a **strict NIH-removing swap** — no behavior change, just less code to maintain.

**No correction to D1–D4, D6–D11.** All other locked decisions stand verbatim.

---

## Implementation Hints for Planner

**Wave organization** (refines CONTEXT.md "Suggested Plan Slicing"):

- **Wave 1 — parallel scaffolding + content authoring**
  - **07-01:** mkdocs-material scaffolding — `mkdocs.yml`, `docs/index.md`, theme config, `pyproject.toml` `[project.optional-dependencies] docs`. Establishes the nav structure that 07-03 / 07-04 / 07-05 plug into.
  - **07-02:** ADR collection (9 MADR ADRs + `0000-template.md` verbatim copy from MADR tag 4.0.0). Pure content. No code touches. Can run in parallel with 07-01 because the nav slot for ADRs is auto-listed by mkdocs (no per-file `nav:` entry needed).

- **Wave 2 — depends on Wave 1 nav, all three parallel-safe with each other**
  - **07-03:** variable reference generator. Adds `settings-doc == 4.3.2` to `[project.optional-dependencies] docs`. Ships `scripts/generate-variables-doc.py` (wrapper) + `scripts/check-variables-drift.sh`. Adds `docs-drift` job to ci.yml (8th job — 7 existing + 1 new). Wires `docs/reference/variables.md` into the nav established by 07-01.
  - **07-04:** migration guide move (`docs/guides/v1-to-v2.md` → `docs/migration/v1-to-v2.md`) + polish + examples/ corpus + examples/migration-v1-to-v2/. Adds `check-jsonschema == 0.37.3` to `dev` group. Adds `tests/structural/test_examples_lint.py`. Critical: re-check all cross-links after the file move — strict-mode build will fail on broken links.
  - **07-05:** docs.yml workflow + mike v1 + v2 publish + GitHub Pages enablement runbook. Adds workflow file, NO new pyproject deps. Documents the `gh api ... pages` command in `docs/admin/repo-settings.md` section 5. **`mike deploy v1` is OUT of CI scope** — runs one-shot from maintainer machine (pitfall #6 above).

- **Wave 3 — additive, independent**
  - **07-06:** README badge polish (sponsors / stars / open issues — additive only, no reorder of existing 7-badge row) + SECURITY.md 6-month wording sharpen + CONTRIBUTING.md verification (no edit if already correct) + CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md verification.

- **Wave 4 — release ceremony prep (depends on Waves 1–3 merged)**
  - **07-07:** PR body template listing every maintainer manual step (mirroring Phase 6 `docs/admin/repo-settings.md` shape). New `docs/admin/repo-settings.md` sections 5 (Pages enablement), 6 (`mike set-default v2`), 7 (`mike deploy v1` one-shot), 8 (Bitbucket Pipe Marketplace listing update), 9 (Docker Hub README deprecation banner — per D10 carryover). Does NOT cut the v2.0.0 tag — Yves runs `git tag v2.0.0 && git push --tags` after merge.

**Verification hints for the verifier (Phase 7 verifier):**
- `mkdocs build --strict` MUST exit 0 against the new docs tree.
- `mike list` after running the planned `mike deploy v2 latest` command MUST show `[v2, latest]`.
- The 8th ci.yml job (`docs-drift`) MUST run and pass.
- `check-jsonschema --builtin-schema vendor.bitbucket-pipelines examples/**/bitbucket-pipelines.yml` MUST exit 0.
- `docs/adr/0000-template.md` MUST be byte-identical to MADR upstream blob `08dac30ed895cf728fc7da95f9702ca4dd5ab900` (sanity-check via `git hash-object docs/adr/0000-template.md` — note: git hash includes the file header so the byte comparison is what matters, not the git hash directly).
- `SECURITY.md` MUST contain the literal placeholder `2026-MM-DD (= v2.0.0 release date + 6 months) — replace at tag-cut.` per D10.

---

## Don't Hand-Roll

| Problem | Don't Build | Use Instead | Why |
|---|---|---|---|
| Pydantic Settings → Markdown | hand-rolled reflector | `settings-doc == 4.3.2` | MIT, pydantic v2 native, battle-tested, ≤30 LOC wrapper |
| `bitbucket-pipelines.yml` schema validation | custom YAML parser | `check-jsonschema --builtin-schema vendor.bitbucket-pipelines` | Apache-2.0, SchemaStore-backed, project-pattern-compatible |
| ADR template authoring | invent our own structure | MADR 4.0 `adr-template.md` verbatim | Industry-standard; CC0; tag-pinned |
| mike publish wrapper | custom git push script | plain `run:` step calling `uv run mike deploy --push --update-aliases v2 latest` | No third-party action to vet; matches upstream guidance |
| gh-pages concurrent deploys | manual retry loop | workflow-level `concurrency: { cancel-in-progress: false }` | Native GitHub Actions feature, queues correctly |
| Docs site versioning | custom `/v1/` and `/v2/` symlinks | `mike == 2.2.0` | Single source of truth (gh-pages branch); mkdocs-material native integration |

---

## Common Pitfalls (referenced earlier in Q10)

1. **mike + concurrent CI deploys → gh-pages race** — mitigation: workflow-level `concurrency:` group with `cancel-in-progress: false`.
2. **mkdocs-material maintenance mode** — no action needed for v2.0; tracked as DOC-NEXT-01.
3. **GitHub Pages private-repo caveat** — N/A (repo is public).
4. **`generate-variables-doc.py` ordering before `mkdocs build --strict`** — document explicitly in docs.yml comment.
5. **`mike set-default v2` is one-shot, not workflow-repeatable** — document as maintainer-runbook step.
6. **`mike deploy v1` is one-shot from local machine, not from CI** — document as maintainer-runbook step.
7. **`id-token: write` MUST NOT be requested in docs.yml** — Phase 6 pitfall #1 carries forward.
8. **MADR template CC0 vs project Apache-2.0** — compatible; no license fragment needed in `0000-template.md`.

---

## Code Examples

### Recommended `pyproject.toml` additions (consolidated)

```toml
[project.optional-dependencies]
docs = [
    "mkdocs-material == 9.7.6",
    "mike == 2.2.0",
    "settings-doc == 4.3.2",
]

[dependency-groups]
dev = [
    # existing entries unchanged ...
    "check-jsonschema == 0.37.3",
]
```

### Recommended docs.yml outline (Plan 07-05)

```yaml
name: docs

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
    tags: ['v*']
  workflow_dispatch:

permissions:
  contents: write  # to push to gh-pages branch
  # id-token: NOT requested — docs.yml does no OIDC signing.

concurrency:
  group: docs-deploy-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: false  # queue, don't cancel — see pitfall #1

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-24.04
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683  # v4.2.2
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0  # mike needs full history for gh-pages branch ops

      - uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@caf0cab7a618c569241d31dcd442f54681755d39  # v3.2.4
        with:
          version: "0.11.21"

      - run: uv python install 3.13
      - run: uv sync --extra docs --frozen

      # Order matters — generator must precede build (pitfall #4).
      - run: uv run scripts/generate-variables-doc.py
      - run: uv run mkdocs build --strict

      - name: Configure git for mike
        run: |
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"

      - name: mike deploy v2 + latest alias
        run: uv run mike deploy --push --update-aliases v2 latest
```

### Recommended `docs-drift` job for ci.yml (Plan 07-03)

```yaml
docs-drift:
  name: "docs-drift (variables.md autogen)"
  runs-on: ubuntu-24.04
  steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683  # v4.2.2
    - uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@caf0cab7a618c569241d31dcd442f54681755d39  # v3.2.4
      with:
        version: "0.11.21"
    - run: uv python install 3.13
    - run: uv sync --extra docs --frozen
    - name: Regenerate variables.md and detect drift
      run: bash scripts/check-variables-drift.sh
```

(Mirrors the Phase 6 `trivy-image` / `pip-audit` job shape — declarative, single-purpose, fail-fast.)

---

## State of the Art

| Old Approach | Current Approach | When Changed | Impact |
|---|---|---|---|
| `mkdocs-material` Insiders behind sponsor paywall | All Insiders features public in `9.7.0` | 2025-11-11 | We get features that previously cost €15/mo for free. No code change needed. |
| `mkdocs-material` feature development | maintenance mode through Nov 2026; Zensical succeeds | 2025-11-06 (#8523) | DOC-NEXT-01 already tracks; ADR-0008 ships with this rationale. |
| `pip install mike` global | uv-managed `[project.optional-dependencies] docs` | Phase 1 src-layout (2026-06) | uv-native, no global state pollution. |
| Hand-rolled "settings → docs" reflectors | `settings-doc` / `pydantic-settings-export` PyPI tools | 2024-09 / 2026-04 | NIH-Verbot win; we use `settings-doc`. |
| `bitbucket-pipelines` lint via online validator only | `check-jsonschema --builtin-schema vendor.bitbucket-pipelines` (offline + CI-gateable) | check-jsonschema 0.37.3 (2026-06-12) | Offline-first lint, no Atlassian SaaS dependency in CI. |

**Deprecated / outdated:** `ktomk/pipelines` PHP CLI (AGPL, last release 2023) — do not adopt.

---

## Environment Availability

> Phase 7 is content + workflow heavy; CI runs entirely on GitHub-hosted `ubuntu-24.04`. No external services required at build time.

| Dependency | Required By | Available on runner? | Version | Fallback |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `uv` | All steps (Python env) | via `astral-sh/setup-uv` action | 0.11.21 (existing pin) | — |
| Python 3.13 | All steps | via `uv python install 3.13` | 3.13 | — |
| Git | mike push | preinstalled | latest stable on `ubuntu-24.04` | — |
| `mkdocs-material` 9.7.6 | docs build | installed via `uv sync --extra docs` | 9.7.6 | — |
| `mike` 2.2.0 | versioned deploy | installed via `uv sync --extra docs` | 2.2.0 | — |
| `settings-doc` 4.3.2 | variable reference gen | installed via `uv sync --extra docs` | 4.3.2 | hand-roll fallback (rejected per NIH-Verbot) |
| `check-jsonschema` 0.37.3 | examples lint | installed via `uv sync` (dev group) | 0.37.3 | — |
| `gh` CLI | maintainer runbook (Pages enable + branch protection) | maintainer local machine | latest stable | manual via GH UI |
| SchemaStore CDN | check-jsonschema schema fetch | online during CI run | n/a | check-jsonschema caches schemas; first run requires net |

**Missing dependencies with no fallback:** none.
**Missing dependencies with fallback:** none required.

## Validation Architecture

### Test Framework

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Framework | `pytest` 9.1 (existing Phase 1 pin) |
| Config file | `pyproject.toml` `[tool.pytest.ini_options]` |
| Quick run command | `uv run pytest tests/structural -k "examples_lint or mkdocs_build or adr_template"` |
| Full suite command | `uv run pytest tests/unit tests/structural --cov=src/aws_eks_helm_deploy --cov-branch --cov-fail-under=100` |

### Phase Requirements → Test Map

| Req ID | Behavior | Test Type | Automated Command | File Exists? |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| DOC-01 | README has badge row + 60s quickstart + docs-site link | structural | `pytest tests/structural/test_readme_polish.py` | ❌ Wave 1 (new) |
| DOC-02 (SC-1) | mkdocs builds strict-mode-clean | structural | `pytest tests/structural/test_mkdocs_build.py` | ❌ Wave 1 (new) |
| DOC-02 (SC-2) | `variables.md` matches autogen output | CI gate | `bash scripts/check-variables-drift.sh` | ❌ Wave 2 (new) |
| DOC-02 (SC-3) | `mike list` shows v1 + v2 | manual / runbook | post-deploy verification step in `docs/admin/repo-settings.md` | runbook only |
| DOC-03 | Migration guide covers every breaking change | structural | `pytest tests/structural/test_migration_guide.py` | ❌ Wave 2 (new) |
| DOC-04 | 9 ADRs follow MADR 4.0 template structure | structural | `pytest tests/structural/test_adr_template.py` | ❌ Wave 1 (new) |
| DOC-05 | CONTRIBUTING.md mentions uv / pre-commit / pytest / kind | structural | `pytest tests/structural/test_contributing.py` | ❌ Wave 3 (new) |
| DOC-06 | SECURITY.md contains 6-month placeholder + 90-day flow | structural | `pytest tests/structural/test_security_md.py` | ❌ Wave 3 (new) |
| DOC-07 | CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md references Contributor Covenant 2.1 | structural | `pytest tests/structural/test_code_of_conduct.py` | ❌ Wave 3 (new) |
| DOC-08 | `examples/{basic,oidc-only,oci-chart,multi-env}/bitbucket-pipelines.yml` lint-clean | structural | `pytest tests/structural/test_examples_lint.py` | ❌ Wave 2 (new) |
| MIG-03 | `examples/migration-v1-to-v2/{before,after}.yml` lint-clean + README.md present | structural | same as above + `test_examples_lint.py::test_migration_pair_present` | ❌ Wave 2 (new) |

### Sampling Rate

- **Per task commit:** `uv run pytest tests/structural -q --no-cov`
- **Per wave merge:** `uv run pytest tests/unit tests/structural --cov-fail-under=100`
- **Phase gate:** full suite green + `docs-drift` ci.yml job green + `mkdocs build --strict` green.

### Wave 0 Gaps

- [ ] `tests/structural/test_readme_polish.py` — covers DOC-01
- [ ] `tests/structural/test_mkdocs_build.py` — covers DOC-02 SC-1
- [ ] `tests/structural/test_migration_guide.py` — covers DOC-03
- [ ] `tests/structural/test_adr_template.py` — covers DOC-04
- [ ] `tests/structural/test_contributing.py` — covers DOC-05
- [ ] `tests/structural/test_security_md.py` — covers DOC-06
- [ ] `tests/structural/test_code_of_conduct.py` — covers DOC-07
- [ ] `tests/structural/test_examples_lint.py` — covers DOC-08 + MIG-03

- [ ] `scripts/check-variables-drift.sh` — CI drift gate

(Test framework itself — `pytest` 9.1 — is already pinned in `pyproject.toml`; no install step needed.)

## Security Domain

### Applicable ASVS Categories

| ASVS Category | Applies | Standard Control |
|---|---|---|
| V2 Authentication | yes (CI surface) | `id-token: write` NOT requested in docs.yml; least-privilege `permissions:` block |
| V5 Input Validation | yes (examples/) | `check-jsonschema vendor.bitbucket-pipelines` validates all `examples/**/bitbucket-pipelines.yml` |
| V6 Cryptography | no | Docs site does not handle secrets |
| V14 Configuration | yes (mkdocs / mike / Pages) | `mkdocs build --strict`; mike concurrency group; Pages branch pinned to `gh-pages` |

### Known Threat Patterns for `.github/workflows/docs.yml`

| Pattern | STRIDE | Standard Mitigation |
|---|---|---|
| Workflow with `pull_request_target` + `checkout` of PR HEAD | Tampering / EoP | docs.yml uses `push` + `workflow_dispatch` only, NOT `pull_request_target` |
| Stale gh-pages history (mike force-push) | Repudiation | mike uses fast-forward; concurrency group prevents races (pitfall #1) |
| Unpinned third-party action | Supply chain / Tampering | every action pinned to 40-char SHA (Q5) |
| Schema-store fetch over insecure transport | Tampering | check-jsonschema fetches over HTTPS; schema cached locally after first run |
| Docs site rendering arbitrary user-submitted Markdown | XSS | N/A — docs are repo-authored, no user input |

---

## Assumptions Log

| # | Claim | Section | Risk if Wrong |
|---|---|---|---|
| A1 | Zero additional mkdocs plugins needed for Phase 7 content (D3, D4, D7 surveyed) | Q3 | Low — if a mermaid block or include directive turns out to need a plugin, planner adds one without rework. |
| A2 | mkdocs-material 9.7.6 keeps the pymdownx mermaid superfences config working | Q3 | Low — pattern is documented in mkdocs-material since 8.x. |
| A3 | `settings-doc` 4.3.2 output format matches the existing README variable-table style enough that the migration guide doesn't need re-wording | Q7 | Medium — if output diverges visibly, the variable-reference page may need a one-time `settings-doc` template override (supported by the tool). |
| A4 | mike 2.2.0 supports `--update-aliases` with mkdocs-material 9.7.6 nav structure | Q2 | Low — combo is widely deployed. |
| A5 | `actions/checkout v4.2.2` is suitable for docs.yml (same as Phase 6 ci.yml pin) | Q5 | Low — Dependabot will bump uniformly. |

---

## Project Constraints (from CLAUDE.md)

The user-global CLAUDE.md surfaces several constraints that bind Phase 7 research:

- **NIH-Verbot:** never invent a mechanism when a maintained open-source standard exists. Applied: `settings-doc` swap (Correction C2), `check-jsonschema` over `ktomk/pipelines`, MADR template verbatim copy.
- **Code, commits, PRs, ADRs, READMEs, docstrings are English.** Applied: all ADR template + migration guide polish work stays in English (the existing draft is already English).
- **Conventional Commits.** Phase 7 commit prefix for docs work: `docs(07): …`; for code work (generator scripts + docs.yml): `feat(07): …` or `chore(07): …`.
- **No new top-level runtime deps without explicit user confirmation.** Phase 7 adds only optional/dev dependencies (`docs` extra + `dev` group `check-jsonschema`). No runtime `pyproject.toml [project].dependencies` change. Consistent with CONTEXT D11.
- **Pin every GitHub Action to a 40-char SHA.** Applied: Q5.
